subject: Product Management
title: Before Product-Market Fit
description: A short lesson to help you apply Before Product-Market Fit.

hook.text: Before PMF, everything a startup does should focus on one thing: finding product-market fit. Nothing else matters.

buildup.text: Hiring aggressively, scaling marketing, or optimizing processes before PMF wastes money and creates false confidence.

discovery.text: Paul Graham calls this phase 'the wilderness' — the chaotic search for something people desperately want.

twist.text: Many startups die here not from running out of money, but from running out of patience and pivoting too late or too often.

climax.text: The pre-PMF discipline is simple but brutal: talk to users, build fast, measure honestly, and iterate relentlessly.

punchline.text: Before PMF, your only job is finding PMF.

quiz.question: What should a startup focus on before achieving PMF?
options: Only finding product-market fit — nothing else matters
Scaling the team as fast as possible
